programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: Tina goes too far trying to be a perfect mentor. Meanwhile, Bob and Linda test out the gym next door.

programme.title: Utopia (AU)
programme.desc: Tony is forced to step in when an endangered grass threatens to derail plans for a new container terminal. Rhonda is convinced that the NBA needs a major overhaul of its website.

programme.title: The Cat Returns
programme.desc: After helping a cat, a seventeen-year-old girl finds herself involuntarily engaged to a cat Prince in a magical world where her only hope of freedom lies with a dapper cat statuette come to life.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Ch. Insp. Conway wants to see P.C. Smith and W.P.C. Martella about a special assignment: going through the missing persons register and following up some of the cases. A cynical Sgt. Penny tells them the initiative is politicially-motivated. A woman who has been harassing her local MP about her missing daughter only wants her back so she can get a three bedroom house. The girl turns up dead. The wife of a missing man who has been onto the Chief Constable is found to have killed herself after seeing his ad in a lonely hearts magazine. Smith is quite excited about his new role, and is hoping to set up a specialised missing persons unit at Sun Hill, but as soon as the politically-sensitive cases are solved, Smith and Martella's unit is disbanded.

programme.title: MythBusters
programme.desc: The team take another look at: salami rocket fuel, rough road driving, pottery record, tailgate up or down, and sword vs sword. Some retests are needed, and some went perfectly, but the fans had more questions.
